title: thymus pulegioides subsp. pulegioides
intro: Thymus pulegioides subsp. pulegioides, commonly known as wild thyme or creeping thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Thymus pulegioides subsp. pulegioides include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief and improving sleep quality. Herbal preparations of Thymus pulegioides subsp. pulegioides include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
